title: Top 10 Herbal Teas for Relieving Frozen Shoulder Pain
intro_desc: Herbal teas for frozen shoulder are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with this condition. They work by reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and improving blood circulation. The best herbal teas for frozen shoulder include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Achillea millefolium, Camellia sinensis, Capsicum annuum, Arnica montana, Cinchona officinalis, Artemisia absinthium, and Clematis vitalba. If left untreated, frozen shoulder can severely impact daily life, limiting mobility and causing chronic pain, making everyday tasks a struggle and affecting overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tea, also known as turmeric tea, is a natural remedy that may help with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move the arm. The active ingredient in turmeric tea, called curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint. This can make it easier to move the arm and perform daily activities. Additionally, curcumin has antioxidant properties that can help protect the joint from further damage and promote healing. Some studies have shown that curcumin can also help to reduce the production of substances that cause inflammation, which can contribute to frozen shoulder. Drinking turmeric tea may help to reduce the severity of frozen shoulder symptoms, such as pain and stiffness, and improve mobility and range of motion in the shoulder joint.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint.
Polyphenols are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility.

Tannins are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling, and also improve blood flow to the affected area, which can help restore mobility and reduce stiffness.

Demethoxycurcumin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint, making it easier to move and regain flexibility.

Flavonoids are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, making it easier to move and recover from the condition.

remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal teas for frozen shoulder
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal tea that helps with frozen shoulder, you'll need dried Angelica root and some basic kitchen tools. First, boil some water in a kettle and let it cool for a minute or two. Next, take one teaspoon of dried Angelica root and add it to a teapot or a mug. Pour the cooled water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired tea strength. You can also add a little honey or lemon to taste, but be sure not to add too much, as it may alter the medicinal properties of the tea. After steeping, strain the tea into another container using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. Drink the tea warm, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help reduce inflammation and ease the pain associated with frozen shoulder. It's essential to note that while Angelica archangelica tea may provide relief, it's not a substitute for medical treatment, and you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy that may help relieve frozen shoulder symptoms. Frozen shoulder, or adhesive capsulitis, is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful. Ginger tea contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The tea may also warm up the muscles and improve blood circulation, making it easier to move the shoulder joint. Additionally, ginger has natural analgesic properties, which may help numb the pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. Drinking ginger tea may also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can contribute to frozen shoulder symptoms. Some people find that adding ginger tea to their daily routine can help improve their range of motion and reduce their reliance on pain medication.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling, allowing the shoulder joint to move more freely.

Shogaols are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the affected shoulder joint, making it easier to move and regain range of motion.
Polyphenols are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected joint, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for frozen shoulder
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal tea to help with frozen shoulder, start by gathering dried or fresh Angelica roots. If you're using fresh roots, rinse them thoroughly and dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. For every 8 ounces of boiling water, add one teaspoon of dried roots or three teaspoons of fresh roots. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Once the tea has cooled, you can drink it as is or add honey or lemon to taste. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of Angelica archangelica tea per day, ideally between meals. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using this tea, especially if you're taking any medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, may help relieve pain and stiffness in frozen shoulder. The tea contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When inflammation occurs in the shoulder joint, it can lead to pain and stiffness. Glycyrrhizin may help reduce inflammation, allowing the joint to heal and move freely again. This can make it easier to perform daily activities, like reaching or lifting. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin may also help reduce the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. Some people find that drinking licorice root tea helps reduce pain and stiffness in the shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily tasks. Regular consumption of the tea may also help improve range of motion and reduce pain over time.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for frozen shoulder
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica archangelica herbal tea that can help with frozen shoulder, you'll need some dried Angelica roots or fresh Angelica leaves. Start by boiling 1 liter of water, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add 2-3 teaspoons of dried Angelica roots or a handful of fresh leaves to the water, depending on your preference for strength. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a light yellow color and the flavor is rich and earthy. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can drink the tea hot or cold, sweetened with honey if you prefer. Some people find it helpful to drink the tea 2-3 times a day, but it's essential to talk to a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.  Note: While Angelica archangelica has been used traditionally to help with frozen shoulder, there is limited scientific evidence to support its effectiveness. Consult a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, is a popular herb used to make tea that can help with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move the arm. The anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow tea may help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint. Yarrow tea also contains antioxidants that can help repair damaged tissues and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, yarrow tea may help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. Start by rinsing the angelica root with cold water to clean it. If using fresh angelica, chop it into smaller pieces. Place the angelica root in the tea infuser and put it in a pot of boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the tea infuse for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a little honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Strain the tea into a cup and drink it while it's still warm. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of angelica tea per day to help ease frozen shoulder pain. You can also store the dried angelica root in an airtight container to make more tea as needed. Note that angelica tea may interact with certain medications, so it's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using it to treat frozen shoulder.

Begin by boiling water in the pot, then add a teaspoon of dried Angelica root for every 8 ounces of water. Allow the mixture to simmer for 5-7 minutes, releasing the herbal properties from the root. As you drink the Angelica archangelica tea, take note of any improvements in your mobility and range of motion, and adjust the frequency or dosage of the tea as needed to find relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.

supplementary_best: For frozen shoulder, a combination of Zingiber officinale, Arnica montana, and Camellia sinensis is recommended. Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural anti-inflammatory that helps reduce pain and stiffness in the shoulder. Arnica montana, or mountain arnica, has anti-inflammatory properties and promotes healing of damaged tissues. It also reduces swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder. Camellia sinensis, or green tea, has anti-inflammatory compounds called polyphenols, which may help reduce pain and inflammation in the shoulder. The combination of these herbs may work together to reduce pain and inflammation in the shoulder, promoting healing and recovery.
supplementary_avoid: If you have frozen shoulder, it's best to avoid herbal teas that may make the condition worse. One such herb is Peppermint (Mentha piperita). Peppermint tea can cause the blood vessels to dilate, which may increase blood flow to the affected area. This increased blood flow can lead to more pain and inflammation, making it harder to move the shoulder.  Another herb to avoid is Ginger-like herbs like  'Glehnia littoralis', or 'Glehnia root'. Glehnia root tea may also increase blood flow to the affected area, leading to more pain and inflammation.  You should also avoid Thyme (Thymus vulgaris) and Oregano (Origanum vulgare) teas. These herbs contain compounds that may irritate the muscles and joints, making the frozen shoulder worse.  Eucalyptus (Eucalyptus globulus) and Rosemary (Rosmarinus officinalis) teas should also be avoided. These herbs can cause the blood vessels to constrict, reducing blood flow to the affected area. This reduced blood flow can make it harder for the body to heal the shoulder.  Lastly, avoid Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia) tea if you have frozen shoulder. While lavender is often used to calm the mind and body, it may cause the muscles to relax too much, leading to more stiffness and limited mobility in the affected shoulder.
supplementary_other_remedies: People with frozen shoulder can try using cold or warm compresses to help reduce pain and stiffness. Applying a cold compress for 15-20 minutes can help reduce inflammation, while a warm compress can help relax the muscles and improve blood flow. Some people also find relief by using heat therapy, such as a warm bath or shower, to loosen up their joints. Epsom salt baths can be especially helpful, as the magnesium in the salt can help reduce inflammation and relax muscles. Another natural remedy that may help is using a foam roller or a tennis ball to gently massage the shoulder area, helping to break up adhesions and improve range of motion. Some people also find relief by using arnica oil or cream, which contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and pain. Additionally, some people with frozen shoulder have found relief by using a device called a TENS unit, which sends electrical impulses to the muscles to help block pain signals.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with frozen shoulder often experience other painful conditions. One common issue is osteoarthritis, a condition where the joints wear down and become inflamed. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which reduces inflammation and eases pain in the joints. Ginger is another herb that can help, as it has anti-inflammatory properties and can reduce swelling in the joints.  Another condition people with frozen shoulder may experience is bursitis, an inflammation of the fluid-filled sacs that cushion the joints. Willow bark contains a compound called salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the bursae.  Some people with frozen shoulder may also experience tendinitis, an inflammation of the tendons that connect muscles to bones. St. John's Wort is a herb that can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the tendons, while also promoting healing and reducing scar tissue.  Additionally, people with frozen shoulder may experience muscle spasms and stiffness, which can be helped by herbs such as passionflower and valerian root. Passionflower can reduce muscle spasms and promote relaxation, while valerian root can help reduce stiffness and promote a good night's sleep.  Lastly, people with frozen shoulder may experience anxiety and depression, which can be helped by herbs such as ashwagandha and lavender. Ashwagandha is an adaptogen that can help reduce stress and anxiety, while lavender can promote relaxation and calmness.  These herbs can be used in various forms, such as teas, tinctures, or topical creams, to help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder and other related conditions.
